title: Decision Flowchart for Using Quantifiers in Logic
description: A flowchart that guides the process of selecting and applying the universal (∀) or existential (∃) quantifier to a logical statement based on the claim being made about a set.
elements: Start: Define a propositional function P(x) and a domain
Decision: Is the claim 'For ALL x in the domain, P(x) is true'?
Decision: Is the claim 'There EXISTS at least one x in the domain for which P(x) is true'?
relationships: The start leads to the first decision point
A 'Yes' answer to the first decision leads to the Universal Quantifier element
A 'No' answer to the first decision leads to the second decision point
A 'Yes' answer to the second decision leads to the Existential Quantifier element
Both quantifier elements lead to the end state
